rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Cloud/remote features carry a Git dependency — out of scope.
Local scheduled tasks catch up on wake.
Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting, not after.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Module file makes no concrete capability assertions beyond plan-mode LO framing at line 14 ('review multi-step agent actions before they run') — accurate general description, no behavior-specific claim to verify.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Known correct Claude Code facts (easy to get wrong): Plan mode = Shift+P (or Shift+Tab cycle).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Module file references plan mode only at the LO level (line 14); key-binding specifics live in the exercise files, not here. No contradiction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit of work — write 'start a new session in X,' not 'open Claude Code in X.'
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Line 19: 'Start a new Claude Code session at your training-directory root.' Correct phrasing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chain prompts by back-reference AND deterministic path.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight.
Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths, never naked filenames.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact-realism: every named tool, skill, connector, file, or path must exist.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The Debrief body prose ('Read Claude's summary. Push back on anything...') is contextual framing and narration, not a forcing function. The forcing function ('When you're done, tell me in 3-5 lines...') lives inside the fenced prompt block.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verification machinery needs a smoke-test before being trusted.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No verification machinery built in this module. Rule 36 applies when Module N constructs a hook/judge/eval and immediately relies on it downstream.
Module correctly uses ./CLAUDE.md as auto-load tier (created in Debrief); memory/ as explicit-load. No phantom auto-load claims. Tier progression is pedagogically sound for M2.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Start a new Claude Code session at your training-directory root.
Use Claude Code's plan mode to review multi-step agent actions before they run
Connectors let the agent combine clues that normally live apart: a wiki page, a document, a meeting note, an email thread, and a useful source from the open web.
